title: Free Will and the Physics of Choice
question: If every particle in your brain obeys physical law, in what sense could you ever have chosen otherwise?
summary: Neuroscience and physics both seem to squeeze out room for a traditional, uncaused free will, yet the felt experience of deliberation and the practical necessity of holding people responsible refuse to disappear. The disagreement may be less about facts than about what 'free' was ever supposed to mean.
